pages.full_text:
Simon + Eve paddled hard to the nearest beach with William swimming behind. Peter's shoes reappeared when they tipped up the boat. We got back + had lunch on a shady piece of land quite near the river. A van came at the end of the meal + a man working for the electricity company warned that the water level of the river would soon be rising.
	We have seen a heron, kingfisher, large fish, minnows, lizards, a deer, many coppiced sweet chestnuts (ground for flour).
	For supper we went to the Hotel de la Vallee. Very good, veg. soup, kiche, rabbit (lapin de lard) cooked with bacon, chips, salad + choc. cake, but service was again very slow. It started raining during the meal. Lightening accompanied us all the way home.
	In the afternoon we went to a pottery shop - Poterie du Son - lovely, expensive pots. We bought a cream jug, dark blue, russet + gold.
	The cheeses here are delicious.
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread. Right margin has partial text visible at gutter belonging to right page. 'kiche' may be 'quiche'. 'Hotel de la Vallee' is in France given context (Conques area, Aveyron).

pages.full_text:
Thursday 7th August
	The campsite now has a second washing machine. I did 4 loads of wash. A glorious day for drying. We played ping pong + made wasp traps + swam.
	The best wasp trap we have seen was at Conques.

__Cross section__	[diagram: hanging string, trough for juice, central hole wasps fly in]

	We have booked for the camp supper this evening "Tripoux". Simon + is having tripe, the rest of us biftek.
	At 8.0. we went down to the "barn" next to the pool - the north-facing wall is missing. It was a super evening. The people organising it were generous with the drink. We started with cassis, then redwine or rose + then a spirit. We had bread, tuna + rice salad, pork + a lovely slice of apple tart. 6F for adults, 3F for children. At the end of the meal the children played outside by the
pages.route_sketch_description: A small hand-drawn cross-section diagram of a wasp trap, labelled with 'hanging string' at the top, 'trough for juice' in the middle, and 'central hole wasps fly in' at the bottom. Shows a bowl-like shape with a central funnel entry point.
pages.notes: Text cuts off at the bottom mid-sentence ('the children played outside by the'). 'Thursday 7th August' year cannot be confirmed from this page alone; within volume A (1989-2003), 7th August fell on a Thursday in 1997 and 2003 among others. 'Tripoux' is a traditional French dish from Aveyron, consistent with Conques (Aveyron, southern France). 'Simon + is having' - word after '+' may be cut off or illegible, possibly a name.
